2. `pnpm vitest run packages/shared` passes — write tests in packages/shared/src/__tests__/color.test.ts covering:
   - parseHexColor: valid #RGB, valid #RRGGBB, invalid inputs throw TypeError
   - pickContrastColor: white bg picks dark contrast, dark bg picks light contrast
   - extractHue: red=#ff0000→0, green=#00ff00→120, blue=#0000ff→240, achromatic=#808080→0
   - buildBrandingCssVars: returns correct CSS string, empty string for falsy input
3. packages/shared/src/index.ts re-exports all 4 functions + RgbColor type
4. No new dependencies added to packages/shared/package.json

title: Fix: recovery OTP form Verify button not in form-actions row — inconsistent with recovery form layout (from ePDS-aq2.5)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.5 found: The fac3f8d commit added a .form-actions row to renderRecoveryForm() (wrapping the Back link + Submit button in a space-between flex row), but renderOtpForm() was not updated consistently.

In renderOtpForm():
- Line 312: <button type="submit" class="btn-primary">Verify</button> is a bare button directly inside the <form>, with no .form-actions wrapper
- Lines 314-322: The .otp-links div (Resend Code + Back to sign in) is outside the form, below it

This means the OTP page has a different layout from the recovery form page:
- Recovery form: [Back to sign in] [Send Recovery Code] — space-between row via .form-actions
- OTP form: [Verify] (full-width? no — width:auto but no row), then [Resend Code] [Back to sign in] below

The .btn-primary has width:auto (set in fac3f8d), so the Verify button will be auto-sized but left-aligned with no counterpart on the left. The spec (ePDS-aq2.5) shows the OTP page should have the Resend/Back links below the Verify button, which is the current structure — but the Verify button should be right-aligned or the layout should be consistent.

Fix: Either wrap the Verify button in a .form-actions div (with the Resend/Back links as the left side), or document that the OTP page intentionally uses a different layout. If the intent is to match the recovery form's form-actions pattern, the otp-links should move inside a .form-actions wrapper alongside the Verify button.

title: Fix: csrfToken accepted in renderLoginPage opts but never used (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The renderLoginPage function accepts `csrfToken: string` in its opts type (line 204) and the route handler passes `csrfToken: res.locals.csrfToken` (line 185), but the csrfToken is never used anywhere in the HTML template or JavaScript.

Evidence:
- Line 204: `csrfToken: string` in opts type
- Line 185: `csrfToken: res.locals.csrfToken` passed to renderLoginPage
- No occurrence of csrfToken in the template body (grep confirms 0 uses in HTML/JS)
- The better-auth fetch calls (/api/auth/email-otp/send-verification-otp, /api/auth/sign-in/email-otp) do not include any CSRF header

Note: This is not a security hole because better-auth routes are mounted before the CSRF middleware (index.ts line 31 vs line 39), so they bypass CSRF validation entirely. However, the dead parameter is misleading and should either be used (if CSRF protection is desired for better-auth routes) or removed from the opts type.

Fix: Remove `csrfToken` from the renderLoginPage opts type and the call site, since it is unused. If CSRF protection for better-auth endpoints is desired in the future, that should be a separate issue.

title: Fix: recovery .link-btn has padding:0 — touch target too small on mobile (from ePDS-aq2.5)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.5 found: The .link-btn CSS rule in recovery.ts (line 636) sets padding: 0, which means the touch target for 'Back to sign in' and 'Resend Code' links is only the text height (~20px). WCAG 2.5.5 recommends a minimum 44×44px touch target.

Evidence:
- recovery.ts line 636: padding: 0; (inside .link-btn rule)
- The same issue exists in login-page.ts (line 565: padding: 0)

The .link-btn elements appear in:
1. .form-actions row: 'Back to sign in' link (line 246)
2. .otp-links row: 'Resend Code' button and 'Back to sign in' link (lines 319, 321)

These are the only interactive elements in those rows, so small touch targets are a real usability issue on mobile.

Fix: Add padding: 8px 0 (or similar) to .link-btn to increase the touch target height to at least 36px, while keeping the visual appearance the same. This fix should be applied to both recovery.ts and login-page.ts for consistency. Note: this is a pre-existing issue in login-page.ts that was copied into recovery.ts.

title: Fix: dark mode body background overridden by light-mode rule ordering (from ePDS-wae.2)
description: The CSS has two body background rules: (1) body { background: var(--color-contrast-0); } at line ~364 and (2) @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { body { background: white; } } at line ~371. In dark mode, rule (1) correctly uses --color-contrast-0 which resolves to hsl(265 20% 6%) — a near-black. However, when AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR is set, the bgColorStyle injects a <style>body { background: ... !important; }</style> block. The !important correctly overrides both rules. But if a client's background_color is set via OAuth metadata (b.background_color), it is NOT injected into the bgColorStyle block — it is only set via --color-panel (which only affects the panel, not body). The body background_color from client OAuth metadata is silently ignored. Document this limitation or implement it.

title: Fix: .layout min-width:100vw causes horizontal scrollbar (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The .layout CSS rule (line 338) sets `min-width: 100vw`. On pages with a vertical scrollbar, `100vw` includes the scrollbar width, causing the layout to be wider than the viewport and triggering a horizontal scrollbar.

Evidence:
- Line 338: `min-width: 100vw;` on .layout
- The Bluesky reference (layout-title-page.tsx) uses Tailwind class `min-w-screen` which maps to `min-width: 100vw` — however, Bluesky's app is a SPA that controls scrollbar behavior. For a server-rendered page with potential content overflow, this causes a horizontal scrollbar on Windows/Linux where scrollbars take up space.

Fix: Replace `min-width: 100vw` with `width: 100%` on .layout. The body already has `min-height: 100vh` and `display: flex`, so .layout will naturally fill the available width.

title: Fix: autofocus on email input fires when OTP step is initially shown (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The email input (line 749) has `autofocus` unconditionally. When `initialStep === 'otp'` (i.e. a login_hint was provided), the email step div is hidden (`class="step-email hidden"`) but the autofocus attribute still fires on the hidden input. This means:
1. The OTP code input does not receive focus on initial load (the JS auto-send fires but doesn't call .focus() on the code input for the initial server-rendered OTP step)
2. Some browsers may scroll to or attempt to focus the hidden email input

Evidence:
- Line 749: `<input ... autofocus` — unconditional
- Line 738: `class="step-email${opts.initialStep === 'otp' ? ' hidden' : ''}"` — hidden when OTP step
- Line 972-984: JS auto-send block does NOT call `document.getElementById('code').focus()` after the OTP send succeeds on initial load

Fix: Make autofocus conditional: only add the autofocus attribute when `opts.initialStep !== 'otp'`. When initialStep is 'otp', add autofocus to the code input instead, or call .focus() in the JS auto-send success handler.

title: Fix: dark mode panel background not applied when AUTH_PANEL_COLOR is set (from ePDS-wae.10)
description: When AUTH_PANEL_COLOR is set, the rootStyle injects --color-panel:${panelColor} into :root inline style. The .title-panel background is set to var(--color-panel) in the >=768px media query (line 415). However, the dark mode override at line 430-434 only adds a border-right — it does NOT override the background. In dark mode with a custom panel color, the panel will show the custom color (correct), but the dark mode :root overrides --color-contrast-25 to a dark value. Since --color-panel defaults to var(--color-contrast-25), without a custom panel color the dark mode panel is correctly dark. With a custom panel color it is correctly the custom color. This is actually fine. BUT: the light-mode rule at line 436-441 uses var(--color-panel, hsl(...)) with a fallback — this fallback is redundant since --color-panel is always defined in :root. Minor: remove the redundant fallback to avoid confusion.

title: Security: client metadata brand_color/background_color not validated — CSS injection via OAuth client (from pre-deploy review)
description:
## Problem

Pre-deployment review found that `brand_color` and `background_color` from OAuth client metadata (fetched from `client_id` URL) are injected into CSS without hex-color validation.

In `packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts`:
```ts
// line 219-220
const brandColor = b.brand_color || opts.defaultBrandColor || '#8338ec'
const bgColor = b.background_color || opts.defaultBgColor || null

// line 239-240 — injected into style attribute
rootStyleProps += `--color-primary:${escapeHtml(brandColor)};...`

// line 250-252 — injected into <style> block
const bgColorStyle = bgColor
  ? `<style>body { background: ${escapeHtml(bgColor)} !important; }</style>`
  : ''
```

`escapeHtml()` only escapes HTML entities (`&`, `<`, `>`, `"`). It does NOT prevent CSS injection via `;`, `}`, `url(`.

A malicious OAuth client at `https://evil.com/client-metadata.json` could set:
```json
{ "background_color": "red} body::after{content:url(https://evil.com/x" }
```

## Mitigations already in place
- CSP `img-src 'self' data:` blocks external image exfiltration
- CSS custom properties in style attributes cannot contain `url()` in most browsers
- The env var path (AUTH_BRAND_COLOR etc.) IS already validated by `isValidHexColor()` (fixed in ePDS-3fc)

## Severity
P2 — requires a malicious OAuth client registration. CSP mitigates the worst exfiltration vectors. Not a deployment blocker.

## Fix
Apply the same `isValidHexColor()` validation to `b.brand_color` and `b.background_color` before using them in CSS. Fall back to defaults if invalid.

title: Fix: success admonition color fails WCAG AA contrast in light mode (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: --color-success: rgb(23 204 136) renders at 1.95:1 contrast ratio against the admonition background (rgba(23,204,136,0.08) on white ≈ rgb(236,250,245)) in light mode. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text (14px). The 'Code resent!' success message is the only user-visible text using this color.

Evidence:
- .admonition.success { color: var(--color-success); background: rgba(23, 204, 136, 0.08); }
- rgb(23,204,136) on rgb(236,250,245) = 1.95:1 contrast ratio
- WCAG AA minimum for text < 18px = 4.5:1
- Dark mode is fine: 8.41:1 on dark admonition background

Fix: darken --color-success for light mode, e.g. use a darker green like rgb(15,140,93) which achieves ~4.6:1 on white, or add a @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) override for --color-success in the admonition context.

title: Fix: overflow:hidden on .input-container clips focus ring (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The .input-container CSS rule has `overflow: hidden` (line 466) which clips the `box-shadow` focus ring defined in .input-container:focus-within (line 477).

Evidence:
- Line 466: `overflow: hidden;` on .input-container
- Line 477: `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--color-primary), 0 0 0 3px var(--color-contrast-0);` on :focus-within
- box-shadow is not clipped by overflow:hidden in most browsers, BUT the outer ring (3px) may be partially clipped depending on browser rendering. More critically, the .btn-primary also has overflow:hidden (line 600) which clips its focus-visible outline.

The .btn-primary:focus-visible rule (line 609) sets `outline: 2px solid var(--color-contrast-1000); outline-offset: 2px;` but the `overflow: hidden` on .btn-primary (line 600) will clip this outline in some browsers.

Fix: Remove `overflow: hidden` from .input-container and .btn-primary. The border-radius clipping can be achieved with `border-radius` alone on child elements, or use `clip-path` only when needed. The focus ring must be visible for WCAG 2.4.7 compliance.

Fix: recovery link passes pdsPublicUrl instead of real request_uri (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 (da62854) found: the recovery link in renderLoginPage uses opts.pdsPublicUrl as the request_uri parameter instead of the actual PAR request_uri.

Evidence (login-page.ts line 827):
  <a href="/auth/recover?request_uri=${encodeURIComponent(opts.pdsPublicUrl)}">

The recovery route (recovery.ts) uses this request_uri to:
1. Create a new auth_flow row if the cookie is missing (line 88) — with wrong requestUri
2. Render a 'Back to sign in' link: /oauth/authorize?request_uri=<value> — this will be the PDS base URL, not a valid PAR request_uri, so clicking 'Back to sign in' from the recovery page will fail with 'Missing request_uri' or show a broken flow

The ui-design branch (the spec reference) correctly adds requestUri to the renderLoginPage signature and passes opts.requestUri to the recovery link. The spec said 'no new parameters' but explicitly listed only defaultBrandColor/defaultBgColor/defaultPanelColor — requestUri was omitted from that exclusion list.

Fix: add requestUri to renderLoginPage opts (matching ui-design branch), pass ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl's actual request_uri from the route handler, and use opts.requestUri in the recovery link href.

title: Restyle renderLoginPage() to match atproto OAuth UI design
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify) — rewrite the CSS and HTML inside renderLoginPage(), keep all JS logic unchanged

## Reference
The target design is the atproto oauth-provider-ui package at:
  /home/kzoeps/Projects/atproto/packages/oauth/oauth-provider-ui/
Key reference files:
  - src/style.css — CSS custom property color token system
  - src/components/layouts/layout-title-page.tsx — split-panel layout
  - src/components/forms/input-container.tsx — pill input styling
  - src/components/forms/button.tsx — button styling
  - src/components/forms/form-card.tsx — form layout
  - src/components/utils/icons.tsx — SVG icon paths (AtSymbolIcon, TokenIcon, EmailIcon)

## What to do

### 1. CSS Custom Properties (color tokens)
Replace the current ad-hoc colors with a CSS custom property system matching atproto's approach:
- \`--color-primary\`: derived from the branding \`brand_color\` param (default: \`#8338ec\` / rgb(131 56 236) — atproto's purple)
- \`--color-primary-contrast\`: white by default
- A neutral contrast scale (--color-contrast-0 through --color-contrast-1000) using HSL with 20% saturation, keyed to the primary hue
- Semantic tokens: \`--color-text-default\`, \`--color-text-light\`, \`--color-border-default\`
- Dark mode: \`@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)\` block that inverts the contrast scale (contrast-0 becomes darkest, contrast-1000 becomes lightest)
- The existing \`brandColor\` and \`bgColor\` from client branding should override \`--color-primary\` and body background respectively via inline style on \`<html>\` or \`:root\`

### 2. Layout — Split Panel
Replace the current centered-box layout with atproto's split-panel:
- On desktop (min-width: 768px): left panel (50% width) with background \`--color-contrast-25\`, right panel with the form content. Left panel contains the logo, "Sign in" title (styled with \`--color-primary\`), and subtitle. Right panel has the form, max-width ~600px, padded.
- On mobile: stacked vertically. Left panel becomes a top bar with logo + title in a row, then form content below.
- Left panel desktop: right-aligned text, subtle right border in dark mode (\`--color-contrast-200\`)
- Body background: white in light mode, \`--color-contrast-0\` (near-black) in dark mode

### 3. Input Styling — Pill Inputs with Icons
Replace bordered inputs with atproto's pill-input pattern:
- Container: \`min-height: 48px; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--color-contrast-25); overflow: hidden;\` (no border)
- Dark mode container: \`background: var(--color-contrast-50)\`
- Inner layout: flexbox row with icon (24px, centered, \`--color-text-light\`), input field, optional append
- Icon turns \`--color-primary\` on focus (use \`:focus-within\` on the container)
- Focus ring on container: \`box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--color-primary);\` with 1px offset (use outline or box-shadow)
- Input itself: \`background: transparent; border: none; outline: none; width: 100%; font-size: 16px; color: var(--color-text-default);\`
- Transition: \`transition: all 0.3s ease-in-out\` on the container

### 4. Email Input
- Icon: AtSymbolIcon SVG (path: \`M12 4a8 8 0 1 0 4.21 14.804....\` — copy the full path from the reference icons.tsx, the AtSymbolIcon export). Render as inline \`<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="20" height="20" fill="currentColor">\`
- Input: \`type="email"\`, same attributes as current
- Label: above the input container as a \`<label>\` with \`font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--color-text-light); margin-bottom: 4px;\`

### 5. OTP Input
- Icon: TokenIcon SVG (path: \`M4 5.5a.5.5 0 0 0-.5.5v2.535...\` — copy from reference). Same inline SVG pattern.
- Keep the monospace, large-letter-spacing, centered-text styling for the OTP code input, but wrap it in the pill container with the icon
- The OTP input should still have: \`font-family: 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 28px; letter-spacing: 8px; text-align: center;\`

### 6. Button Styling
- Primary button: \`background: var(--color-primary); color: var(--color-primary-contrast); border: none; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 24px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.3s ease-in-out;\`
- Focus ring: \`outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary); outline-offset: 2px;\`
- Disabled: \`opacity: 0.5\`
- Secondary/text buttons (Resend, Back): \`background: var(--color-contrast-25); color: var(--color-text-light); border: none; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 24px;\` Dark mode: \`background: var(--color-contrast-50)\`
- Button row: flex row-reverse with space-between (primary action on the right, cancel/back on the left), matching atproto's \`flex-row-reverse\` pattern

### 7. Social Login Buttons
- Restyle to match: pill-shaped (\`border-radius: 8px\`), \`background: var(--color-contrast-25)\`, icon + text, hover darkens slightly
- Divider: use \`--color-border-default\` for the line color, \`--color-text-light\` for text

### 8. Error Display
- Match atproto's Admonition pattern: \`border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--color-border-default); display: flex; gap: 8px;\` with an alert icon SVG on the left
- Error text color: use a red/error semantic color (define \`--color-error: rgb(255 0 110)\`)
- Success messages (code resent): use \`--color-success: rgb(23 204 136)\`

### 9. Recovery Link
- Style as subtle text link: \`color: var(--color-text-light); font-size: 13px; text-decoration: none;\` with hover underline

### 10. Dark Mode
- All of the above must work in dark mode via \`@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)\`
- Body: dark background
- Inputs: slightly lighter than body
- Text: light colors
- Left panel: darker shade with right border

## Don't
- Do NOT change any JavaScript logic (sendOtp, verifyOtp, step transitions, event listeners, auto-send on login_hint)
- Do NOT change the form field names, IDs, or data flow
- Do NOT add any external dependencies (no Tailwind, no CSS framework, no npm packages)
- Do NOT change the route handler logic (flow creation, cookie setting, client metadata resolution)
- Do NOT modify any other files besides login-page.ts
- Do NOT add i18n/localization
- Do NOT use \`import\` for CSS — everything stays as inline \`<style>\` in the template literal (this is server-rendered HTML)
- Do NOT change the renderError() function at the bottom of the file
acceptance_criteria:
1. The login page renders with a split-panel layout: left panel (branding/title) + right panel (form) on viewports >= 768px; stacked vertically on mobile
2. Inputs use pill-shaped containers (rounded, gray background, no border) with SVG icons on the left (AtSymbolIcon for email, TokenIcon for OTP)
3. Input containers show a primary-color focus ring when the inner input is focused
4. Primary buttons use --color-primary background with --color-primary-contrast text; secondary buttons use gray background
5. Dark mode activates via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) — body goes dark, text goes light, inputs use slightly lighter background, left panel has right border
6. CSS custom properties are used for all colors (--color-primary, --color-contrast-*, --color-text-*, --color-error, --color-success)
7. Client branding overrides (brand_color, background_color, logo_uri) still work — they set the CSS custom properties
8. All existing JS functionality is preserved: email submit → OTP send, OTP verify → redirect, resend, back button, auto-send on login_hint, idempotency (otpAlreadySent)
9. Social login buttons (Google/GitHub) render correctly when configured
10. pnpm build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
11. pnpm typecheck passes
close_reason: f49b056 restyle renderLoginPage() to match atproto OAuth UI design

title: Add PDS-level branding env vars for auth login page
description: ## Files - packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify) - packages/auth-service/src/context.ts (modify) — add new config fields - packages/auth-service/src/index.ts (modify) — read env vars into config - packages/auth-service/.env.example (modify) — document new vars ## What to do Add 4 new env vars for PDS-level default branding on the auth login page: \`\`\` AUTH_LOGO_URL=https://gainforest.earth/_assets/media/7155311d31a04b4cf1242b10b11062e6.png AUTH_BRAND_COLOR=#3E7053 AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR=#F2ECE2 AUTH_PANEL_COLOR=#3E7053 \`\`\` ### 1. Context / config changes In \`src/context.ts\`, add these optional fields to the config type: \`\`\`ts logoUrl?: string brandColor?: string backgroundColor?: string panelColor?: string \`\`\` In \`src/index.ts\` \`main()\`, read from env: \`\`\`ts logoUrl: process.env.AUTH_LOGO_URL || undefined, brandColor: process.env.AUTH_BRAND_COLOR || undefined, backgroundColor: process.env.AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR || undefined, panelColor: process.env.AUTH_PANEL_COLOR || undefined, \`\`\` ### 2. Login page changes In \`login-page.ts\`, the \`createLoginPageRouter\` function already has access to \`ctx\`. Pass the config branding into \`renderLoginPage()\` as new options: \`\`\`ts defaultLogoUrl: ctx.config.logoUrl, defaultBrandColor: ctx.config.brandColor, defaultBgColor: ctx.config.backgroundColor, defaultPanelColor: ctx.config.panelColor, \`\`\` Then in \`renderLoginPage()\`, change the priority chain: \`\`\`ts // Priority: client metadata > env var defaults > hardcoded fallbacks const brandColor = b.brand_color || opts.defaultBrandColor || '#8338ec' const bgColor = b.background_color || opts.defaultBgColor || null const panelColor = opts.defaultPanelColor || null // no client metadata equivalent const logoUrl = b.logo_uri || opts.defaultLogoUrl || null \`\`\` For the logo HTML: \`\`\`ts const logoHtml = logoUrl ? \`\` : '' \`\`\` ### 3. Panel color CSS The left panel (\`.title-panel\`) currently uses \`background: var(--color-contrast-25)\` on desktop. When \`panelColor\` is set, override this with the solid color. Add a new CSS custom property \`--color-panel\` that defaults to \`var(--color-contrast-25)\` but can be overridden: - In the \`:root\` block: \`--color-panel: var(--color-contrast-25);\` - In the \`rootStyle\` inline override: if panelColor is set, add \`--color-panel: ${panelColor};\` - The \`.title-panel\` desktop rule uses \`background: var(--color-panel);\` When the panel has a dark background color (like #3E7053), the title text and subtitle need to be light. Add a CSS custom property \`--color-panel-text\` that defaults to \`var(--color-primary)\` for the title and \`var(--color-text-light)\` for the subtitle. When panelColor is set, override both to white: - \`--color-panel-text: #fff;\` (for title h1) - \`--color-panel-subtitle: rgba(255,255,255,0.8);\` (for subtitle) Update the CSS: - \`.title-panel h1 { color: var(--color-panel-text, var(--color-primary)); }\` - \`.title-panel .subtitle { color: var(--color-panel-subtitle, var(--color-text-light)); }\` Only set these overrides in the rootStyle when panelColor is provided. To determine if text should be light, check if the panel color is dark. A simple heuristic: parse the hex color, compute relative luminance, if < 0.5 use white text. Or just always use white text when panelColor is explicitly set (since the user chose a custom color, they presumably want contrast). ### 4. Background color The \`bgColor\` (from AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR or client metadata) should set the body background. The existing \`bgColorStyle\` mechanism already handles this — it injects a \`\` block. Just make sure the env var default feeds into this. ### 5. .env.example Add to the "Auth-service only" section: \`\`\` # PDS-level branding defaults (overridden by OAuth client metadata if present) # AUTH_LOGO_URL=https://example.com/logo.png # AUTH_BRAND_COLOR=#8338ec # AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR=#F2ECE2 # AUTH_PANEL_COLOR=#3E7053 \`\`\` ## Don't - Do NOT change any JavaScript client-side logic (sendOtp, verifyOtp, etc.) - Do NOT change form field names, IDs, or data flow - Do NOT add npm dependencies - Do NOT change the renderError() function - Do NOT break existing client metadata branding — it must still take priority over env vars
acceptance_criteria:
1. When AUTH_LOGO_URL is set, the logo appears on the login page (no client metadata logo needed)
2. When AUTH_BRAND_COLOR=#3E7053 is set, buttons use that green color
3. When AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR=#F2ECE2 is set, the body/right-panel background is warm beige
4. When AUTH_PANEL_COLOR=#3E7053 is set, the left panel on desktop is solid green with white text
5. When a client provides brand_color/background_color/logo_uri in its metadata, those take priority over env vars
6. When no env vars and no client metadata are set, the atproto purple fallback (#8338ec) is used
7. The .env.example documents all 4 new vars
8. pnpm build succeeds
9. pnpm typecheck passes

title: Fix: client background_color override broken by body background in light mode (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The client branding `background_color` override is applied as `background` on the `<html>` element via `rootStyle`, but the `body` has an explicit `background: white` rule inside `@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)` that takes precedence (body > html for background painting). As a result, any client-supplied `background_color` is silently ignored in light mode. Evidence: login-page.ts line 335-337 sets `body { background: white; }` in a light-mode media query, overriding the html-level inline style. Fix: apply the `background_color` override directly to `body` via a `<style>` block or move the override to a CSS custom property consumed by body.
close_reason: 2bc9fac fix background_color override: move from html inline style to body-targeted style block after light-mode media query

title: Fix: --color-text-light uses wrong contrast step (contrast-600 vs spec contrast-700) (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The semantic token `--color-text-light` is set to `var(--color-contrast-600)` (43.6% lightness) but the atproto reference style.css (line 232) defines it as `var(--color-contrast-700)` (34.2% lightness). This makes secondary text (labels, subtitles, placeholder text) slightly too light, reducing contrast and diverging from the reference design. Evidence: login-page.ts line 297 vs atproto style.css line 232. Fix: change `--color-text-light: var(--color-contrast-600)` to `--color-text-light: var(--color-contrast-700)`.
close_reason: 2bc9fac fix: --color-text-light uses contrast-700 per atproto spec

title: Fix: social login buttons remain visible during OTP step (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The social login buttons (Google/GitHub) and their surrounding dividers are rendered outside the step-email/step-otp divs and are never hidden when the user transitions to the OTP step. When a user clicks 'Continue with email' and moves to the OTP step, the social buttons remain visible above the OTP form, which is confusing and inconsistent with the atproto reference UI. Evidence: login-page.ts lines 708-709 — `${socialButtonsHtml}` is placed unconditionally in the form-panel, outside both step divs. Fix: either wrap socialButtonsHtml in a div with class `step-email` so it hides with the email step, or hide it explicitly in `showOtpStep()` / show it in `showEmailStep()`.

title: Fix: success admonition uses wrong icon (alert triangle instead of checkmark) (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The admonition element (#error-msg) always renders an alert triangle SVG icon regardless of whether it is showing an error or a success message. When `showSuccess('Code resent!')` is called, the element switches to the `.success` CSS class (green border/background) but the icon remains the warning triangle, which is semantically incorrect and visually jarring. Evidence: login-page.ts lines 699-706 — the alert triangle SVG is hardcoded inside the admonition div. Fix: either use two separate icon spans (one for error, one for success) toggled by JS, or replace the icon SVG via JS in `showError()`/`showSuccess()`.
close_reason: 2d58468 fix: success admonition shows checkmark icon instead of alert triangle

title: Fix: color-mix() used for admonition backgrounds has limited browser support (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The admonition error/success background uses `color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-error) 8%, transparent)` (login-page.ts lines 649, 655). `color-mix()` is not supported in Safari < 16.4 (March 2023) or Firefox < 113 (May 2023). Users on older browsers will see no background tint on the admonition, which is a minor visual regression but still a compatibility gap. The spec did not require `color-mix()` — it only specified `border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--color-border-default)`. Fix: replace with a hardcoded low-opacity rgba fallback or use a CSS custom property with a pre-computed tint value.
close_reason: a140492 fix: replace color-mix() with rgba fallbacks for admonition backgrounds

title: Fix: justify-content: stretch is invalid in flexbox (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The `.layout` desktop media query sets `justify-content: stretch` (login-page.ts line 352). `stretch` is not a valid value for `justify-content` in flexbox (it is valid for `align-content` and in CSS Grid). Browsers silently ignore this declaration, so the layout falls back to the default `justify-content: flex-start`. This means the right form panel does not expand to fill remaining horizontal space on very wide viewports — the form panel stays at its `max-width: 600px` and the remaining space is empty. Fix: remove `justify-content: stretch` or replace with `justify-content: flex-start` (the default). The left panel's `width: 50%` and the right panel's `width: 100%` already handle the layout correctly without this property.
close_reason: 845b8fa fix: remove invalid justify-content: stretch from flexbox layout

title: Fix: OTP input missing autocomplete='email' on email field (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The email input (login-page.ts line 722) has `type="email"` but is missing `autocomplete="email"`. Without this attribute, password managers and browser autofill may not correctly identify the field as an email address field, reducing UX especially on mobile. The atproto reference uses `autocomplete` attributes on all form fields. The OTP input already correctly has `autocomplete="one-time-code"`. Fix: add `autocomplete="email"` to the email input element.
close_reason: a97a985 fix: add autocomplete="email" to email input

title: Pixel-perfect polish: align login page CSS with atproto reference
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify) — CSS and HTML tweaks only

## Reference
Compare against the atproto oauth-provider-ui at /home/kzoeps/Projects/atproto/packages/oauth/oauth-provider-ui/

## What to do

The current implementation captures the overall design language but has several pixel-level differences from the atproto reference. Fix all of the following:

### 1. Title panel — desktop layout needs grid centering
The atproto LayoutTitlePage uses a grid inside the left panel to vertically center the content:
\`\`\`css
/* atproto: .title-panel inner wrapper */
display: grid;
align-content: center;
justify-items: end; /* right-aligned on desktop */
\`\`\`
Currently our .title-panel uses flexbox with justify-content:center which doesn't behave the same way. The inner content (logo + h1 + subtitle) should be wrapped in a div with \`display: grid; align-content: center; justify-items: end;\` on desktop. On mobile, the title-panel should use \`justify-items: start\`.

### 2. Title font size — desktop should go larger at lg breakpoint
atproto uses responsive title sizing:
- Mobile: \`text-xl\` = 20px ✓ (we have this)
- md (768px): \`text-2xl\` = 24px (we jump to 40px — too big)
- lg (1024px): \`text-5xl\` = 48px

Fix: Use 24px at md breakpoint, 48px at lg breakpoint (add a 1024px media query).

### 3. Title panel padding
atproto uses:
- Mobile: \`px-6 pt-4\` = padding: 16px 24px (top only, no bottom) ✓ roughly matches
- Desktop: \`p-4\` = 16px all around (we use 32px — too much)

Fix: Desktop padding should be 16px, not 32px.

### 4. Form panel padding
atproto uses:
- Mobile: \`p-6\` = 24px ✓ (we have this)
- Desktop: \`px-12\` = 48px horizontal, but the vertical padding is inherited from p-6 = 24px

Our desktop padding is 48px all around. Fix: desktop should be \`padding: 24px 48px;\`

### 5. Input container — cursor-text class
atproto's InputContainer has \`cursor-text\` on the outer div (so clicking anywhere in the pill focuses the input). Add \`cursor: text;\` to \`.input-container\`.

### 6. Input inner padding
atproto uses \`px-1\` = 4px horizontal padding on the inner flex row. We have \`padding: 0 4px\` which matches. But the icon uses \`mx-2\` = 8px margin on each side. Our icon has \`width: 36px\` as a fixed width. Change to: remove the fixed width, add \`margin: 0 8px;\` to the icon instead.

### 7. Input text styling
atproto's input has: \`text-ellipsis bg-clip-padding\`. Add \`text-overflow: ellipsis;\` and \`background-clip: padding-box;\` to \`.input-field\`.

### 8. Placeholder color in dark mode
atproto uses \`dark:placeholder-gray-500\` which is \`rgb(107 114 128)\`. Our placeholder uses \`var(--color-text-light)\` which is the contrast-700 step. These are close but not identical. Add a dark mode override: \`.input-field::placeholder\` in dark mode should be \`color: rgb(107 114 128);\`

### 9. Button tracking (letter-spacing)
atproto buttons have \`tracking-wide\` = \`letter-spacing: 0.025em;\`. Add this to both \`.btn-primary\` and \`.btn-secondary\`.

### 10. Button touch-manipulation
atproto buttons have \`touch-manipulation\` to eliminate 300ms tap delay on mobile. Add \`touch-action: manipulation;\` to both button classes.

### 11. Form gap should be 16px between fields, 16px before button row
atproto's FormCard uses \`gap-4\` (16px) between the children wrapper and the button row. Our \`.form-group\` has \`gap: 16px\` ✓. But the children wrapper inside uses \`space-y-4\` (16px between fields). Verify our \`.field\` spacing matches — currently \`.field\` has \`gap: 4px\` which is the gap between label and input, not between fields. The gap between fields comes from \`.form-group\`'s gap. This should be correct.

### 12. Button row — exact atproto pattern
atproto uses: \`flex flex-row-reverse flex-wrap items-center justify-end space-x-2 space-x-reverse\`
This translates to:
\`\`\`css
display: flex;
flex-direction: row-reverse;
flex-wrap: wrap;
align-items: center;
justify-content: flex-end;
gap: 8px; /* space-x-2 with space-x-reverse */
\`\`\`
Our current \`.btn-row\` uses \`justify-content: flex-start\` — but since it's row-reverse, flex-start means the right side. Actually this is equivalent to justify-end in a normal row. Verify this is correct. The key difference: atproto uses \`space-x-2 space-x-reverse\` not gap. In practice with our plain CSS, \`gap: 8px\` is fine.

### 13. Subtitle text on the title panel
In atproto, the subtitle says "Enter your username and password" or "Enter your password" etc. Our subtitle says "to use **AppName**". This is fine — it's our own copy. But the subtitle should have \`max-width: 320px\` on desktop (atproto uses \`max-w-xs\` = 320px). We have 280px — change to 320px.

### 14. Logo margin on desktop
atproto: \`mb-2\` on mobile (8px), \`md:mb-4\` (16px) on desktop. We have \`margin-bottom: 8px\` on desktop. Change to 16px.

### 15. Title margin on desktop
atproto: \`md:my-4\` = 16px top and bottom margin on desktop. We have \`margin: 16px 0\` ✓. This matches.

### 16. Body font — ensure system font stack matches
atproto uses Tailwind's default system font stack which is:
\`ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji"\`
We use: \`-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif\`
These are functionally equivalent but for pixel-perfection, use the Tailwind stack.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any JavaScript logic
- Do NOT change form field names, IDs, or data flow
- Do NOT add external dependencies
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Do NOT change the renderError() function
acceptance_criteria:
1. Title panel uses grid centering on desktop (align-content: center, justify-items: end)
2. Title font sizes: 20px mobile, 24px at 768px, 48px at 1024px
3. Title panel desktop padding is 16px
4. Form panel desktop padding is 24px vertical, 48px horizontal
5. Input containers have cursor: text
6. Input icons use margin: 0 8px instead of fixed width
7. Input fields have text-overflow: ellipsis and background-clip: padding-box
8. Buttons have letter-spacing: 0.025em and touch-action: manipulation
9. Subtitle max-width is 320px on desktop
10. Logo desktop margin-bottom is 16px
11. Font stack matches Tailwind default
12. pnpm build succeeds
13. pnpm typecheck passes
